tavily.raw: {"query":"Germany H5N1 poultry farm outbreaks September 2025 affected states agriculture ministry response","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.wattagnet.com/poultry-meat/diseases-health/avian-influenza/news/15774950/europe-ends-2025-with-more-than-700-avian-flu-outbreaks","title":"Europe ends 2025 with more than 700 avian flu outbreaks","content":"To date in 2025, a total of 699 outbreaks of highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) have been recorded in commercial poultry farms in 23 European countries. This is according to the Animal Disease Information System from the European Commission (EC; as of December 24). In the great majority of outbreaks, presence of the H5N1 HPAI virus serotype has been confirmed. Over the past seven days, poultry have tested positive for the H5N1 virus variant at five more farms in each of Germany, Italy, and Poland. In the adjacent state of Lower Saxony, the outbreak total has risen to 79 with the discovery of the virus in a flock of laying hens. In Poland, latest to be hit by the H5N1 virus were two farms in each of the northern province of Pomerania — including one flock of more than 172,000 laying hens — and in central Greater Poland.","score":0.685971,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://beaconbio.org/en/report?reportid=bb1de153-d54a-4672-87c4-c855852ab1e2&eventid=0a0393c4-d899-4b32-8a67-2c192694074d","title":"Germany intensifies poultry control measures amid ...","content":"Oct 27, 2025 — Number of reported HPAIV H5 cases in wild birds, affected bird groups, and locations by federal state between September 1 and October 20, 2025.","score":0.63939893,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.fao.org/animal-health/situation-updates/global-aiv-with-zoonotic-potential/en","title":"Global AIV with Zoonotic Potential","content":"## Animal health. ### **Global Avian Influenza Viruses with Zoonotic Potential situation update**. This update covers avian influenza viruses (AIV) with zoonotic potential occurring worldwide, i.e. H5Nx, H7Nx high pathogenicity avian influenza (HPAI) viruses and H3N8, H5Nx, H6N1, H7Nx, H9N2, H10Nx and H11 low pathogenicity avian influenza (LPAI). Specific information is available for Avian Influenza A(H7N9) virus-virus/en) viruses and Sub-Saharan Africa HPAI  in related FAO Avian Influenza situation updates. **HPAI outbreaks in animals** officially reported since last update (23 April 2026): in total, 763 outbreaks / events have been reported in 31 countries/territories caused by H5Nx (95), H5N1 (659), H5N5 (3), H5N9 (3) and H7N3 (3) (see Table 1 for details). **Number of human cases** officially reported since the last update: 5 new events were reported. **Map 2.** *Global distribution of AIV with zoonotic potential\\* observed in the period 1 October 2024 to 30 September 2025 (i.e. previous wave)*. **Table 1.**High pathogenicity avian influenza viruses with zoonotic potential reported **since the last update**.","score":0.32562825,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/worldanimalhealth/posts/july-2025-saw-a-relatively-low-number-of-avian-influenza-or-bird-flu-outbreaks-i/1155266729966625","title":"July 2025 saw a relatively low number of avian influenza ...","content":"since October 2021, more than 58 million birds have died or been culled in H5N1-affected poultry establishments in 37 European countries.","score":0.24958482,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12712863","title":"Avian influenza overview September–November 2025 - PMC","content":"An official website of the United States government.
